11: From paper tally sheets to live farm telemetry. Inurum built the mobile and backend infrastructure powering Fruitrack's harvest monitoring across thousands of workers.

Agricultural enterprises managing dozens of harvest teams had no reliable way to track worker output in real time. Supervisors reconciled handwritten tallies at end-of-day — hours after corrective action could have mattered. Harvest quantities, worker hours, and task completions lived in notebooks, not systems.

The client needed a field-ready mobile platform workers could operate with minimal training, that synced data even under poor connectivity, and that gave farm managers a live dashboard — not a next-morning report.
Harvest monitoring & worker task management for agricultural enterprises.

Inurum architected and delivered Fruitrack end-to-end: Flutter mobile apps for iOS and Android, a synchronisation engine that queues records offline and commits on reconnect, and a web dashboard for farm managers tracking live output across all active teams.
Worker-facing screens were designed for speed — harvesters log a passagem in under 10 seconds. Supervisors get task event logs, timestamps, and quantity totals without leaving the app.
SQLite queue on device; records commit to AWS on connectivity restore — no data loss in low-signal fields.
Web portal gives supervisors real-time harvest totals, worker task logs, and team productivity by parcel.
Single codebase for iOS and Android — consistent UX across device types used by diverse field teams.
Full roster control: active/inactive workers, batch assignment, and individual task history.
Inurum builds mobile and IoT systems that work in the real world — offline-first, cross-platform, and production-grade.
We build products that work in the real world. Based in Indore, MP, India.

12: End-to-end engineering of a plug-and-play remote start and idle management system for North American Class 8 semi-trucks.

Over-the-road drivers in Canada operate in conditions where diesel gels, batteries drain overnight, and a cold engine start risks serious mechanical damage. The standard practice — leave the engine idling for hours, or wake before dawn to warm up manually — was both expensive and increasingly illegal under anti-idling regulations.

The industry had no affordable, warranty-safe aftermarket remote start solution built for heavy-duty diesel trucks. The client arrived with a clear product concept and an open market. They needed a team capable of owning the product from hardware to app to cloud — and shipping something commercially ready.
No warranty-safe aftermarket remote start existed for 2013+ semi-trucks across any of the major OEM brands.
Cold starts and overnight idling cost fleets thousands per truck annually in fuel, engine wear, and compliance risk.
Seven major North American truck manufacturers — each with a proprietary electrical architecture.
Proprietary plug-and-play device with truck-specific OEM adapter harnesses. No factory wiring is cut or spliced. Validates vehicle state before every start sequence.
Cross-platform driver app with remote start, real-time engine status, GPS tracking, alert management, and configurable runtime — accessible from any cellular connection.
Real-time data pipeline for vehicle status, battery voltage, engine temperature, and location — with push notifications and a subscription management layer.
Multi-point pre-trip inspection engine with automated shutdown on critical anomalies. SafeMode hard-disables all remote starts when a technician is present.

Every unit ships with a custom-designed PCB, truck-specific adapter harness, and a ruggedized enclosure engineered to survive the vibration, heat, and electrical noise of a commercial diesel environment.
Start the engine from any location via app. Configurable runtime from 10 minutes to 1 hour.
Engine starts automatically when coolant temperature drops below a driver-defined threshold.
Starts automatically when battery drops below 12V, preventing dead batteries during rest periods.
Real-time location with engine status. Built-in theft recovery capability for fleet operators.
Verifies 6 parameters — oil, coolant, parking brake, gear, battery, hood — before any start.
Continuous monitoring of coolant, oil pressure, and temperature. Auto-shutdown at critical thresholds.
Push notifications for battery voltage, engine status, runtime, and failure diagnostics.
Plug-and-play adapters. No factory wiring splice. Compatible with 2013+ trucks across 7 brands.

Every hard constraint in this project came from the physical world — truck electrical systems, extreme weather, remote locations, and mechanics with wrenches. We solved each one at the engineering layer, not with workarounds.

Seven manufacturers, seven proprietary electrical architectures. We built a library of OEM-specific adapter harnesses so a single firmware base works across all supported models without wiring modifications.

Remotely starting a diesel engine with no driver present required a rigorous multi-sensor validation sequence. Six parameters are verified before initiation; three are monitored continuously during operation.
Drivers park in areas with poor cellular coverage. The communication layer handles intermittent connectivity gracefully — queuing commands, confirming execution, and alerting drivers on failures.

Any factory wiring modification voids the OEM warranty — a hard constraint for fleet operators. Solved entirely at the hardware level using pass-through adapter harnesses that leave factory wiring untouched.

The install had to be accessible to a driver with no electrical background. We redesigned the connector sequence and built a guided flow with live video-call support — achieving under two hours from box-open to first remote start.

We built across embedded firmware, mobile, cloud, and hardware — all under one engineering team. This gave us full control over reliability, safety, and future feature delivery without external dependencies.
We take products from concept to commercial launch — embedded hardware, firmware, mobile apps, and cloud infrastructure, all under one roof.
We build products that work in the real world. Based in Indore, India.

16: An AI multi-sensor IoT system that studies your indoor environment and connects you to the right plants — simplifying cultivation and tracking growth in real time.

ELIoT is a smart living wall system made entirely in Italy by GardenStuff, designed to integrate plants seamlessly into homes, terraces, offices, and shops. Before installing a single plant, ELIoT's IoT sensor conducts a96-hour environmental survey— measuring temperature, light, air humidity, soil moisture, and pH levels every 6 minutes — then recommends the exact species and soil type best suited to that specific space.

Inurum built the companion mobile app that pairs with ELIoT's wireless sensor hardware. The app receives live telemetry, surfaces personalized care plans, and alerts users when watering or fertilizing is needed. It also integrates with voice assistants and smart home systems to automate lighting and climate for optimal plant conditions.
Before recommending a single plant, ELIoT runs a 96-hour environmental analysis — sampling 5 sensor channels every 6 minutes.
Sensor hardware runs up to 6 months between charges, designed for hassle-free indoor living environments.
AI analysis of live sensor data predicts potential plant issues and tracks growth — turning raw telemetry into actionable care guidance.
Custom-designed PCBs purpose-built for soil intelligence — a soil moisture probe, a multi-sensor main board, and a leaf-shaped enclosure with a glowing status indicator.
We leveraged robust and proven technologies to deliver ELIoT's real-time sensor telemetry, cross-platform mobile experience, and AI-driven plant intelligence backend.

17: End-to-end engineering of a connected cold-chain control platform — custom hardware, ESP32 firmware, cellular connectivity, and real-time cloud visibility for refrigeration units in moving vehicles.
Three stages of development — each video shows the system running on real hardware, not a simulation.
Production-ready unit with full cellular cloud connectivity, live temperature telemetry, and remote compressor control.
Initial ESP32 board bring-up with monochrome display — validating sensor reads and UI loop.
First integrated build — compressor relay, cellular modem, and cloud sync running together for the first time.

Mobile refrigeration systems carry critical payloads — food, pharmaceuticals, temperature-sensitive materials — inside moving vehicles operating in dynamic environments. The client's existing units functioned in complete isolation: no visibility, no control, no way to detect or respond to failures remotely.

Every adjustment required manual intervention. Every failure went unnoticed until damage was done. They needed a system that stays connected 24/7, provides full remote control, and ensures operational reliability across every deployed unit — at any scale.
No real-time temperature visibility — spoilage went undetected until delivery.
Every adjustment required a technician on-site. No remote control existed.
No centralized system to manage multiple units across distributed fleets.
Moving vehicles, variable external temperatures, and intermittent cellular coverage.

Dedicated hardware card engineered for mobile refrigeration units. Manages firing cycles, captures temperature data, and executes control logic in real time — built for reliability in variable vehicle conditions.

Embedded firmware on ESP32, optimized for connected IoT environments. Handles continuous temperature monitoring, intelligent cycle control, wireless communication, and automation logic under real-world operating conditions.

Persistent 24/7 connectivity via integrated Quectel communication module. Enables real-time data transmission, remote command execution, and reliable performance in moving vehicle environments — connectivity as a core layer, not an afterthought.
Full-stack platform with real-time monitoring, remote temperature control, automated alerts, live location tracking, and multi-unit fleet management — accessible via mobile app and web dashboard.
Set and adjust temperature from anywhere via mobile app or web dashboard — no technician required on-site.
Continuous temperature telemetry streamed to cloud. Live dashboard shows current state of every deployed unit.
Instant notifications when temperature deviates from defined thresholds — before spoilage occurs.
GPS tracking integrated into the platform. Know where every unit is at all times across the fleet.
Centralized dashboard to monitor, control, and manage multiple refrigeration units simultaneously.
Persistent cellular connection via Quectel ensures continuous data flow even in remote or moving environments.

Every constraint in this project came from the physical world — moving vehicles, dynamic temperatures, remote locations, and payloads that can't afford failure. We solved each at the engineering layer.
Vehicle vibration, power fluctuations, and external temperature swings required hardware and firmware engineered specifically for mobile deployment — not adapted from stationary designs.

Maintaining 24/7 connectivity in moving vehicles with variable signal strength required a communication layer that handles intermittent coverage gracefully — queuing data, confirming delivery, alerting on failures.

Firmware, cloud, and applications had to stay synchronized in real time. Any lag between a temperature event and an operator alert could mean spoilage. The pipeline was engineered for minimal latency end-to-end.
The platform had to manage a single unit on day one and scale to an entire fleet without re-engineering. The cloud backend was designed for horizontal scale from the start.
We owned the full stack — from control hardware and embedded firmware to cellular communication, cloud backend, and user-facing applications. No external dependencies on any critical layer.
We engineer complete systems — control hardware, embedded firmware, cellular connectivity, cloud platforms, and mobile apps — all under one roof.
We build products that work in the real world. Based in Indore, India.
